idea: Automated invoice chasing for freelance designers

evidence.summary: Freelancers repeatedly complain about chasing unpaid invoices and awkward follow-ups.
evidence.quote: I hate chasing late payments every month.

evidence.summary: A designer would pay for reminders that sound human and reduce awkward follow-ups.
evidence.quote: I would pay for something that chases invoices without sounding robotic.

evidence.summary: Existing tools track invoices but do not handle relationship-aware reminders.
Multiple freelancers describe losing billable hours because payment follow-up is manual and emotionally awkward.
evidence.quote: I delay follow-ups because I do not want to sound aggressive.

idea: Client follow-up OS for boutique agencies

evidence.summary: Agency owners lose revenue because manual client follow-up falls through.
evidence.quote: We miss renewals when follow-up lives in inboxes.

evidence.summary: Two agencies accepted a paid pilot if reminders integrate with their existing workflow.
Operators repeatedly mention missed follow-ups, late renewals, and forgotten client check-ins as recurring revenue leaks.
Operators ask for lightweight follow-up systems instead of another CRM.
